event_properties.[Amplitude] Page Title: Embedding Tableau into Salesforce: A guide - The Information Lab
Embedding Tableau into Salesforce: A guide - The Information Lab
event_properties.[Amplitude] Element Class: my-5 text-[15px] font-light
event_properties.[Amplitude] Element Text: Salesforce.com is a market-leading CRM platform, and it recently acquired Tableau, a market-leading data visualisation platform. Many companies are using both tools heavily, and have deeply embedded the two systems into the day-to-day activities.In order to consolidate both platforms, a common question is posed on how to marry these two platforms together. This post will go through a couple of methods of doing this.Update September 2020: There is now another new and easy way to embed Tableau into Salesforce. Learn more about it here!In order to do any of the following, you do require the correct permissions, so do check with your Salesforce administrator.Adding a Web TabThe simplest and most basic option is to add a Tab with an embedded web-page which is linking to Tableau Server. In order to create the Tab, go to the 'Setup' cog in the top right section and search for the 'Tabs' section.
